revision.text:
'''休日ニ関スル件'''（きゅうじつにかんするけん）とは[[休日]]について規定した日本の勅令。
==関連項目== *[[休日]] *[[皇室祭祀令]] *[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]
[[Category:日本の旧法令|きゆうしつにかんするけん]]

'''休日ニ関スル件'''（きゅうじつにかんするけん）とは[[休日]]について規定した日本の勅令。
==概要== [[1914年]]（大正元年）[[9月4日]]に公布され、即日施行された。
対象の休日は元始祭（1月3日） 、新年宴会（1月5日）、紀元節（2月11日）、神武天皇祭（4月3日）、明治天皇祭（7月30日）、天長節（8月31日）、神嘗祭（10月17日）、新嘗祭（11月23日）、春季皇霊祭（春分日）、秋季皇霊祭（秋分日）。
[[1927年]]（昭和2年）3月4日に改正公布され、即日施行された。明治天皇祭（7月30日）、天長節（8月31日）が休日でなくなり、 天長節（4月29日）、明治節（11月3日） 、大正天皇祭（12月25日）が休日に追加された。
[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]により、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]に廃止。
==関連項目== *[[休日]] *[[皇室祭祀令]] *[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]
[[Category:日本の旧法令|きゆうしつにかんするけん]]

'''休日ニ関スル件'''（きゅうじつにかんするけん、休日ニ關スル件）は、[[休日]]について規定した[[日本]]の[[勅令]]。
==概要== [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）[[9月4日]]に公布され、即日施行された。
対象の休日は[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇]]祭（[[4月3日]]）、[[明治天皇]]祭（[[7月30日]]）、[[天長節]]（[[8月31日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]に改正公布され、即日施行された。明治天皇祭（7月30日）、天長節（8月31日）が休日でなくなり、 天長節（[[4月29日]]）、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]） 、[[大正天皇]]祭（[[12月25日]]）が休日に追加された。
[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]により、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]に廃止。
==関連項目== *[[休日]] *[[皇室祭祀令]] *[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]
[[Category:日本の旧法令|きゆうしつにかんするけん]]

[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]に改正公布され、即日施行された。明治天皇祭（7月30日）、天長節（10月31日）が休日でなくなり、 天長節（[[4月29日]]）、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]） 、[[大正天皇]]祭（[[12月25日]]）が休日に追加された。
[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]により、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]に廃止。

[[Category:日本の旧法令|きゆうしつにかんするけん]]

[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]に改正公布され、即日施行された。明治天皇祭（7月30日）が休日でなくなり[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]） 、[[大正天皇]]祭（[[12月25日]]）が休日に追加された。また、天長節が10月31日から[[4月29日]]へ変更された。

対象の休日は[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇]]祭（[[4月3日]]）、[[明治天皇]]祭（[[7月30日]]）、[[天長節]]（[[8月31日]]、[[1914年]]（大正3年）から[[10月31日]]に変更）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]に改正公布され、即日施行された。明治天皇祭（7月30日）が休日でなくなり[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]） 、[[大正天皇]]祭（[[12月25日]]）が休日に追加された。また、天長節が10月31日から[[4月29日]]へ変更された。
[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]により、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]に廃止。

'''休日ニ関スル件'''（きゅうじつにかんするけん、休日ニ關スル件）とは[[休日]]について規定した[[日本]]の[[勅令]]である。
==概要== [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）[[9月4日]]に公布され、即日施行された。
対象の休日は[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇]]祭（[[4月3日]]）、[[明治天皇]]祭（[[7月30日]]）、[[天長節]]（[[8月31日]]、[[1914年]]（大正3年）から[[10月31日]]に変更）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]に改正公布され、即日施行された。明治天皇祭（7月30日）が休日でなくなり[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]） 、[[大正天皇]]祭（[[12月25日]]）が休日に追加された。また、天長節が10月31日から[[4月29日]]へ変更された。
[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]により、[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]に廃止。
==関連項目== {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件 (大正二年)}} *[[休日]] *[[皇室祭祀令]] *[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:きゆうしつにかんするけん}} [[Category:日本の旧法令]] [[Category:休日]] [[Category:戦前日本の祝祭日|*きゆうしつにかんするけん]] [[Category:1912年の法]] [[Category:1948年に廃止された法]]

'''休日ニ関スル件'''（きゅうじつにかんするけん、休日ニ關スル件）とは[[休日]]について規定した[[日本]]の[[勅令]]である。
==概要== [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）[[9月4日]]に公布され、即日施行。同時にそれまでの[[年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム]]は廃止された。
年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ムから[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇]]祭（[[4月3日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）を継承。[[明治天皇]]崩御ならびに[[大正天皇]]即位につき、先帝祭は[[孝明天皇]]祭（[[1月30日]]）から明治天皇祭（[[7月30日]]）に移動、[[天長節]]は[[8月31日]]に移動となった。
[[1913年]]（大正2年）7月16日の改正により、天長節祝日（[[10月31日]]）を追加。大正天皇誕生日の8月31日が盛暑期につき式典の斎行が困難とされ設けられた。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]の改正により、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]）を追加。大正天皇崩御ならびに[[昭和天皇]]即位につき、先帝祭は明治天皇祭から大正天皇祭（[[12月25日]]）に移動、[[天長節]]は[[4月29日]]に移動となり、天長節祝日は廃止された。
[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]、[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]の公布・即日施行と同時に廃止された。
==関連項目== {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件 (大正二年)}} *[[休日]] *[[皇室祭祀令]] *[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:きゆうしつにかんするけん}} [[Category:日本の旧法令]] [[Category:休日]] [[Category:戦前日本の祝祭日|*きゆうしつにかんするけん]] [[Category:1912年の法]] [[Category:1948年に廃止された法]]

==概要== [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）[[9月4日]]に公布され、即日施行。同時にそれまでの[[年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム]]は廃止された。
年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ムから[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇]]祭（[[4月3日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）を継承。[[明治天皇]]崩御ならびに[[大正天皇]]践祚につき、先帝祭は[[孝明天皇]]祭（[[1月30日]]）から明治天皇祭（[[7月30日]]）に移動、[[天長節]]は[[8月31日]]に移動となった。
[[1913年]]（大正2年）7月16日の改正により、天長節祝日（[[10月31日]]）を追加。大正天皇誕生日の8月31日が盛暑期につき式典の斎行が困難とされ設けられた。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]の改正により、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]）を追加。大正天皇崩御ならびに[[昭和天皇]]践祚につき、先帝祭は明治天皇祭から大正天皇祭（[[12月25日]]）に移動、[[天長節]]は[[4月29日]]に移動となり、天長節祝日は廃止された。
[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]、[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]の公布・即日施行と同時に廃止された。

年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ムから[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇]]祭（[[4月3日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）を継承。[[明治天皇]]崩御ならびに[[大正天皇]]践祚につき、先帝祭は[[孝明天皇]]祭（[[1月30日]]）から明治天皇祭（[[7月30日]]）に移動、[[天長節]]は[[8月31日]]に移動となった。
[[1913年]]（大正2年）[[7月16日]]の改正により、天長節祝日（[[10月31日]]）を追加。大正天皇誕生日の8月31日が盛暑期につき式典の斎行が困難とされ設けられた。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]の改正により、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]）を追加。大正天皇崩御ならびに[[昭和天皇]]践祚につき、先帝祭は明治天皇祭から大正天皇祭（[[12月25日]]）に移動、[[天長節]]は[[4月29日]]に移動となり、天長節祝日は廃止された。
[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]、[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]の公布・即日施行と同時に廃止された。

年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ムから[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇祭]]（[[4月3日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）を継承。[[明治天皇]][[崩御]]ならびに[[大正天皇]][[践祚]]に伴い、[[先帝祭]]は[[孝明天皇]]祭（[[1月30日]]）から明治天皇祭（[[7月30日]]）に移動、[[天皇誕生日|天長節]]は[[8月31日]]に移動となった。
[[1913年]]（大正2年）[[7月16日]]の改正（大正2年勅令第259号）により、天長節祝日（[[10月31日]]）を追加。大正天皇[[誕生日]]の8月31日が盛暑期であるため、式典の斎行が困難とされて設けられた。なお、式典の斎行日が移動しただけであり、8月31日が天長節として休日であることに変わりは無く、休日が1日増えることとなった。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]の全部改正（昭和2年勅令第25号）により、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]）を追加。大正天皇崩御ならびに[[昭和天皇]]践祚に伴い、先帝祭は明治天皇祭から大正天皇祭（[[12月25日]]）に移動、天長節は[[4月29日]]に移動となり、盛暑期を理由にした天長節祝日は廃止された。天長節祝日廃止により休日が1日減ってしまうところを明治節で穴埋めする格好になっている。
[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]、[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]（昭和23年法律第178号）附則第2項により、同法の施行と同時に廃止された。

[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]、[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]（昭和23年法律第178号）附則第2項により、同法の施行と同時に廃止された。

revision.text:
{{Japanese law | title = Regarding Holidays | status = Abolished | type = | content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} | related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} | link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927年3月4日] | wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日}}).
== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912年9月4日}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

revision.text:
{{Japanese law | title = Regarding Holidays | status = Abolished | type = | content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} | related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} | link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927年3月4日] | wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日}}).
== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912年9月4日}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==

* [[Jimmu Festival]]

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

{{Japanese law | title = Regarding Holidays | status = Abolished | type = | content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} | related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} | link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927年3月4日] | wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日}}).
== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912年9月4日}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

{{Japanese law | title = Regarding Holidays | status = Abolished | type = | content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} | related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} | link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927年3月4日] | wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日}}).
== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912年9月4日}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

{{reflist}}

{{Japanese law | title = Regarding Holidays | status = Abolished | type = | content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} | related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} | link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927年3月4日] | wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日}}).
== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912年9月4日}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== Related Articles ==
{{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件 (大正元年)}} {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件 (大正二年)}} {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件|休日ニ關スル件 (昭和二年)}} * {{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日}} * {{ill|Public holidays in Japan|ja|祝日}} * {{ill|Festive Days|ja|祭日}} * {{ill|Imperial Household Religious Service Ordinance|ja|皇室祭祀令}} * {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}}

== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912年9月4日}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913年7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913年7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927年3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927年3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} ({{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会}} ({{ill|January 5|ja|1月5日}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節}} ({{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}}), {{ill|Jinmu Emperor Festival|ja|神武天皇祭}} ({{ill|April 3|ja|4月3日}}), {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}), {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} ({{ill|November 23|ja|11月23日}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai}} ({{ill|Spring Equinox|ja|春分}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai}} ({{ill|Autumn Equinox|ja|秋分}}), were retained.
Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|ja|明治天皇}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taisho|ja|大正天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭}} ({{ill|January 30|ja|1月30日}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|ja|7月30日}}), and the {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天皇誕生日}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|ja|8月31日}}.
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日}} ({{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節}} ({{ill|November 3|ja|11月3日}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Showa|ja|昭和天皇}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|ja|12月25日}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|ja|4月29日}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948年7月20日}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

revision.text:
{{日本の法令 |題名 = 休日ニ関スル件 |効力 = 廃止 |種類 = |内容 = [[祝祭日]]の規定 |関連 = [[国民の祝日に関する法律]] |リンク = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927年3月4日] |ウィキソース = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''休日ニ関スル件'''（きゅうじつにかんするけん）は、[[祝祭日]]（[[休日]]）について規定した[[日本]]の[[勅令]]である。
==概要== [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）[[9月4日]]に[[公布]]（大正元年勅令第19号）され、即日[[施行]]。同時にそれまでの[[年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム]]（明治6年太政官布告第344号）は廃止された。
年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ムから[[元始祭]]（[[1月3日]]） 、[[新年宴会]]（[[1月5日]]）、[[紀元節]]（[[2月11日]]）、[[神武天皇祭]]（[[4月3日]]）、[[神嘗祭]]（[[10月17日]]）、[[新嘗祭]]（[[11月23日]]）、[[春季皇霊祭]]（[[春分]]日）、[[秋季皇霊祭]]（[[秋分]]日）を継承。[[明治天皇]][[崩御]]ならびに[[大正天皇]][[践祚]]に伴い、[[先帝祭]]は[[孝明天皇]]祭（[[1月30日]]）から明治天皇祭（[[7月30日]]）に移動、[[天皇誕生日|天長節]]は[[8月31日]]に移動となった。
[[1913年]]（大正2年）[[7月16日]]の改正（大正2年勅令第259号）<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報1913年07月18日]</ref>により、天長節祝日（[[10月31日]]）を追加。大正天皇[[誕生日]]の8月31日が盛暑期であるため、式典の斎行が困難とされて設けられた。なお、式典の斎行日が移動しただけであり、8月31日が天長節として休日であることに変わりは無く、休日が1日増えることとなった。
[[1927年]]（[[昭和]]2年）[[3月4日]]の全部改正（昭和2年勅令第25号）<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報1927年03月04日]</ref>により、[[明治節]]（[[11月3日]]）を追加。大正天皇崩御ならびに[[昭和天皇]]践祚に伴い、先帝祭は明治天皇祭から大正天皇祭（[[12月25日]]）に移動、天長節は[[4月29日]]に移動となり、盛暑期を理由にした天長節祝日は廃止された。天長節祝日廃止により休日が1日減ってしまうところを明治節で穴埋めする格好になっている。
[[1948年]]（昭和23年）[[7月20日]]、[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]（昭和23年法律第178号）附則第2項により、同法の施行と同時に廃止された。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
==関連項目== {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件 (大正元年)}} {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件 (大正二年)}} {{Wikisource|休日ニ關スル件|休日ニ關スル件 (昭和二年)}} *[[休日]] *[[祝日]] *[[祭日]] *[[皇室祭祀令]] *[[国民の祝日に関する法律]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:きゆうしつにかんするけん}} [[Category:廃止された日本の勅令]] [[Category:廃止された日本の法律]] [[Category:戦前日本の祝祭日|*きゆうしつにかんするけん]] [[Category:1912年の法]] [[Category:1948年に廃止された法]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:休日ニ関スル件]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11381150]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Japanese law
| title = Regarding Holidays
| status = Abolished
| type = 
| content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}}
| related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|qq=|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}}
| link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927th year 3月4日]
| wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件
}}
'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|qq=|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|qq=|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|qq=|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|qq=|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|qq=|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|qq=|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|qq=|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|qq=|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

| wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件

'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Japanese law
| title = Regarding Holidays
| status = Abolished
| type = 
| content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}}
| related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}}
| link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927th year 3月4日]
| wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件
}}
'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:休日ニ関スル件]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11381150]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese law | title = Regarding Holidays | status = Abolished | type = | content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} | related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} | link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927th year 3月4日] | wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件 }} '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).
== Overview ==
It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.
The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained.
Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].
On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.
On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.
On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.
