'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ([[:en:January 3|January 3]]), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ([[:en:January 5|January 5]]), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ([[:en:February 11|February 11]]), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ([[:en:April 3|April 3]]), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]), [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] ([[:en:November 23|November 23]]), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Chunfen|Spring Equinox]]), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ([[:en:Qiufen|Autumn Equinox]]), were retained. 

Due to the demise of [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] and the accession of [[:en:Emperor Taishō|Emperor Taisho]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:January 30|January 30]]) to Meiji Emperor Festival ([[:en:July 30|July 30]]), and the [[:en:The Emperor's Birthday|Emperor's Birthday]] (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to [[:en:August 31|August 31]].

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:October 31|October 31]]). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]], the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ([[:en:December 25|December 25]]), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to [[:en:April 29|April 29]]. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ([[:en:November 3|November 3]]). '''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ({{ill|January 3|en|January 3|WD=Q2156|lt=January 3}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ({{ill|January 5|en|January 5|WD=Q2202|lt=January 5}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|February 11|en|February 11|WD=Q2335|lt=February 11}}), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}), {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Chunfen|en|Chunfen|WD=Q1339268|lt=Spring Equinox}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Qiufen|en|Qiufen|WD=Q1200436|lt=Autumn Equinox}}), were retained. 

Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taisho}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|January 30|en|January 30|WD=Q2287|lt=January 30}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|en|July 30|WD=Q2716|lt=July 30}}), and the {{ill|The Emperor's Birthday|en|The Emperor's Birthday|WD=Q72318|lt=Emperor's Birthday}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|en|August 31|WD=Q2830|lt=August 31}}.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|October 31|en|October 31|WD=Q2949|lt=October 31}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ({{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ({{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

{{Japanese law
| title = Regarding Holidays
| status = Abolished
| type = 
| content = {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}}
| related = {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}}
| link = [{{NDLDC|2956510/1}} 官報 1927th year 3月4日]
| wikisource = 休日ニ關スル件
}}
'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ({{ill|January 3|en|January 3|WD=Q2156|lt=January 3}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ({{ill|January 5|en|January 5|WD=Q2202|lt=January 5}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|February 11|en|February 11|WD=Q2335|lt=February 11}}), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}), {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai}} ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Chunfen|en|Chunfen|WD=Q1339268|lt=Spring Equinox}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Qiufen|en|Qiufen|WD=Q1200436|lt=Autumn Equinox}}), were retained. 

Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taisho}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|January 30|en|January 30|WD=Q2287|lt=January 30}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|en|July 30|WD=Q2716|lt=July 30}}), and the {{ill|The Emperor's Birthday|en|The Emperor's Birthday|WD=Q72318|lt=Emperor's Birthday}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|en|August 31|WD=Q2830|lt=August 31}}.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|October 31|en|October 31|WD=Q2949|lt=October 31}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ({{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

'''Regarding Holidays''' (休日に関する件) was an {{ill|Imperial ordinance|ja|勅令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial ordinance|1=imperial ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q67421961}} of Japan that regulated {{ill|Public holidays|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Public holidays|qq=}} ({{ill|Non-working days|ja|休日|1=Non-working days|2=ja|3=休日|4=de|5=Ruhetag (Wirtschaft)|6=el|7=Αργία|8=hi|8=छुट्टी|9=ko|10=쉬는 날|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-working days|qq=}}).

== Overview ==

It was promulgated on {{ill|September 4, 1912|ja|1912nd year 9月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 4, 1912|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Taisho 1, Imperial Ordinance No. 19) and took effect on the same day. Simultaneously, the previous {{ill|Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|ja|年中祭日祝日ノ休暇日ヲ定ム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Proclamation on Yearly Public Holidays and Festivals|1=Establishment of Holidays for Annual Festivals and Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483813}} (Meiji 6, Daijokan Proclamation No. 344) was abolished.

The holidays from the previous proclamation, including {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} ({{ill|January 3|en|January 3|WD=Q2156|lt=January 3|1=January 3}}), {{ill|New Year's Banquet|ja|新年宴会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Banquet|1=Shinnen Enkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502138}} ({{ill|January 5|en|January 5|WD=Q2202|lt=January 5|1=January 5}}), {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|qq=}} ({{ill|February 11|en|February 11|WD=Q2335|lt=February 11|1=February 11}}), {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17|1=October 17}}), {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}}), {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Chunfen|en|Chunfen|WD=Q1339268|lt=Spring Equinox|1=Chunfen}}), and {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} ({{ill|Qiufen|en|Qiufen|WD=Q1200436|lt=Autumn Equinox|1=Qiufen}}), were retained. 

Due to the demise of {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|WD=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} and the accession of {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|WD=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taisho|1=Emperor Taishō}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from {{ill|Emperor Komei Festival|ja|孝明天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Komei Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|January 30|en|January 30|WD=Q2287|lt=January 30|1=January 30}}) to Meiji Emperor Festival ({{ill|July 30|en|July 30|WD=Q2716|lt=July 30|1=July 30}}), and the {{ill|The Emperor's Birthday|en|The Emperor's Birthday|WD=Q72318|lt=Emperor's Birthday|1=The Emperor's Birthday}} (Tenchōsetsu) was moved to {{ill|August 31|en|August 31|WD=Q2830|lt=August 31|1=August 31}}.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|October 31|en|October 31|WD=Q2949|lt=October 31|1=October 31}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ({{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3|1=November 3}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa|1=Hirohito}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25|1=December 25}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.

On {{ill|July 16, 1913|ja|1913rd year 7月16日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 16, 1913|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was amended (Taisho 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 259)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2952391/1 官報 1913rd year 7月18日]</ref> to add a {{ill|Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja|天長節祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu holiday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|October 31|en|October 31|WD=Q2949|lt=October 31|1=October 31}}). The birthday of Emperor Taisho on August 31 was deemed unsuitable for ceremonies due to the summer heat, leading to the establishment of a new ceremonial day while keeping August 31 as a public holiday, thus increasing the number of holidays by one.

On {{ill|March 4, 1927|ja|1927th year 3月4日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 4, 1927|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a full revision (Showa 2, Imperial Ordinance No. 25)<ref>[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2956510/1 官報 1927th year 3月4日]</ref> added the {{ill|Meiji Setsu|ja|明治節|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Setsu|1=Meiji-setsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512398}} ({{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3|1=November 3}}). Following the demise of Emperor Taisho and the accession of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa|1=Hirohito}}, the {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} was moved from the Meiji Emperor Festival to the Taisho Emperor Festival ({{ill|December 25|en|December 25|WD=Q2745|lt=December 25|1=December 25}}), and the Tenchōsetsu was moved to {{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}}. The Tenchōsetsu holiday added in 1913 was abolished due to its summer timing. The Meiji Setsu was introduced to offset this loss, maintaining the total number of holidays.

On {{ill|July 20, 1948|ja|1948th year 7月20日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 20, 1948|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ordinance was abolished with the implementation of the {{ill|Act on National Holidays|ja|国民の祝日に関する法律|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Act on National Holidays|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421011}} (Law No. 178 of 1948), as stipulated in Article 2 of the supplementary provisions of the act.
